report_name: Springfield Hospital Data Breach Exposes Patient Info, Triggers Class-Action Lawsuit Probe

threat_actors.name: Unidentified Actor
threat_actors.description: The unidentified threat actor responsible for the email compromise and subsequent data breach at Springfield Hospital, which was discovered in December 2025.
threat_actors.goals: Theft of patient Protected Health Information (PHI) and Personally Identifiable Information (PII) for financial fraud or identity theft.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: The breach originated from a compromised employee email account, likely through a phishing attack, to gain initial access.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The attacker gained unauthorized access to an employee's email account and operated within it.

attack_patterns.name: Email Collection: Remote Email Collection
attack_patterns.description: The attacker accessed sensitive patient data, including Social Security numbers and medical information, contained within the compromised Microsoft 365 email account.

attack_patterns.name: Email Forwarding Rule
attack_patterns.description: The attacker likely created an inbox rule to automatically forward emails to an external account, as suggested by the 'New-InboxRule' command pattern.

identities.name: Springfield Hospital
identities.description: A critical access hospital in Vermont that was the victim of a data breach via a compromised employee email account.

identities.name: Springfield Hospital Patients
identities.description: Patients of Springfield Hospital whose Protected Health Information (PHI) and Personally Identifiable Information (PII) were exposed in the data breach.

indicators.name: Exchange Inbox Forwarding Rule Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: Exchange Inbox Forwarding Rule Creation
id: 2b1f8c3d-e4a5-4b6c-8d7e-9f0a1b2c3d4e
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of a new inbox rule that forwards emails to an external address. This is a common technique used by attackers to exfiltrate data or maintain persistence after an email account compromise.
references:
  - https://www.classaction.org/springfield-hospital-data-breach
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-15
tags:
  - attack.t1114.003
  - attack.collection
level: high
logsource:
  product: m365
  service: audit.exchange
detection:
  selection:
    Operation: 'New-InboxRule'
    Parameters.ForwardTo|exists: true
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of an email forwarding rule in Microsoft 365, a common TTP for data exfiltration from a compromised mailbox.
